novel_features.name: Firmware summary check
novel_features.description: Show the latest synced firmware version per AXS device type.
novel_features.rationale: Reads fw_version and end_ts from synced quarqnet component summaries.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs the current synced firmware signal without clicking through every component.

novel_features.name: Battery dashboard
novel_features.description: Latest synced battery status per AXS device type, including voltage when available.
novel_features.rationale: Reads battery_status, voltage, and end_ts from synced quarqnet component summaries.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this for a quick pre-ride battery signal across synced AXS device types.

novel_features.name: Component wear rollup
novel_features.description: Rank components by synced shift counts, distance, and actuations from component summaries.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates component summaries across synced activities; shift counts are the wear proxy.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to find the most-used drivetrain component for maintenance planning.

novel_features.name: Ride shift log
novel_features.description: Show per-ride front/rear shift counts, chainrings, and cogs from synced component summaries.
novel_features.rationale: Surfaces start_ts, fd_shift_count, rd_shift_count, num_chainrings, and num_cogs while withholding large gear series unless explicitly requested as JSON.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use for ride-by-ride drivetrain usage analysis without dumping thousands of raw gear samples.

novel_features.name: Unified garage view
novel_features.description: A tree of every bike with its components and serials in one shot.
novel_features.rationale: Joins bikes and components into a single tree no API call returns.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use as the one-command overview of a rider's whole AXS setup.
novel_features.group: AXS web data joined for agents

novel_features.name: Since last sync
novel_features.description: Show new ride activities and notifications since your last sync.
novel_features.rationale: Time-windowed diff over the local mirror; impossible without persistent local state.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this to catch up on what changed without re-reading everything.

narrative.headline: Every SRAM AXS feature from the terminal, plus a local database, offline search, and cross-bike views the web app has no way to show.
narrative.value_prop: Manage registered bikes and components, register parts by serial, check synced firmware and battery summaries, review ride activity data, and roll up shift usage — all scriptable with --json. A local SQLite mirror powers offline search and compounding views like `wear`, `shifts`, and `since`.
narrative.auth_narrative: AXS uses your SRAM account (Auth0). Run `axs-pp-cli auth login` to sign in with your email and password (handled directly against SRAM's token endpoint — no browser), or paste an access token with `axs-pp-cli auth set-token` / set SRAM_AXS_TOKEN. The public device-type catalog works with no login.

quickstart.comment: check reachability and config before anything else
public catalog — works with no login, proves the API is reachable
sign in with your SRAM account to unlock your bikes and components
one-command overview of every bike and component
rank synced component usage from local component summaries
read latest synced battery_status per AXS device type
total front and rear shifts across synced ride summaries

troubleshoots.fix: Run `axs-pp-cli auth login` or set SRAM_AXS_TOKEN to a valid access token.
Run `axs-pp-cli auth login` again; access tokens are short-lived.
Run `axs-pp-cli sync` to populate the local mirror, then retry.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI when a rider or agent needs scriptable access to a SRAM AXS account: listing bikes and components, registering parts by serial, checking synced firmware/battery status, reviewing ride activity data, or querying synced AXS data offline.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI to flash or install firmware on components — it reports versions only; actual updates require the SRAM AXS app over Bluetooth.
Do not use it to configure shifting behavior or pair components over BLE — that is a phone-app/Bluetooth task, not an HTTP API task.
Do not use it for non-SRAM drivetrains.

recipes.title: Pre-ride charge check
recipes.explanation: Lists latest battery_status per synced AXS device type.

recipes.title: Find stale firmware
recipes.explanation: Lists latest fw_version per synced AXS device type.

recipes.title: Rank drivetrain use
recipes.explanation: Ranks component-summary records by shift-count wear proxy.

recipes.title: Total ride shifts
recipes.explanation: Totals front and rear shifts across synced ride summaries.

recipes.title: Register a part safely
recipes.explanation: Shows the registration request without sending it.
